This can occur in (1) belief, e.g. to believe that other than Allah has the power to benefit or harm, (2) speech, e.g. to swear by other than Allah and (3) action, e.g. to bow or prostrate to other than Allah.)(Allahumma innee aAAoothu bika an oshrika bika wa-ana aAAlam, wa-astaghfiruka lima la aAAlam.

title_fr: Invocation contre la peur du shirk (associationnisme)

title_en: Invocation for someone who tells you: May Allah bless you
title_ar: الدعاء لمن قال بارك الله فيك

duas.arabic: ((وَفِيكَ بَارَكَ اللَّهُ)).
duas.translation_en: (Aaishah reported that the Messenger of Allah  was given a sheep and he ordered for it’s distribution.  When the servant would come back (from distributing it), AAaishah would ask: ‘What did they say?’, he replied: They would supplicate: (May Allah bless you all.) AAaishah would then say: (and may Allah bless them.))(we return their supplication in a similar way and our reward remains with us.)
duas.reference: (Aaishah reported that the Messenger of Allah  was given a sheep and he ordered for it’s distribution.  When the servant would come back (from distributing it), AAaishah would ask: ‘What did they say?’, he replied: They would supplicate: (Barakal-lahu feekum.) AAaishah would then say: (Wafeehim barakal-lah.))(we return their supplication in a similar way and our reward remains with us.)
duas.translation_fr: (Aïcha a rapporté que le Messager d'Allah a reçu un mouton et qu'il a ordonné sa distribution. Lorsque le serviteur revenait (de la distribution), AAïsha demandait : « Qu'ont-ils dit ? », il a répondu : Ils invoqueraient : (Qu'Allah vous bénisse tous.) AAaïcha disait alors : (et qu'Allah les bénisse.) (nous répondons à leur supplication de la même manière et notre récompense reste avec nous.)
duas.phonetic: Aaishah reported that the Messenger of Allah  was given a sheep and he ordered for it’s distribution.  When the servant would come back (from distributing it), AAaishah would ask: ‘What did they say?’, he replied: They would supplicate: (Barakal-lahu feekum.) AAaishah would then say: (Wafeehim barakal-lah.))(we return their supplication in a similar way and our reward remains with us.

title_en: Invocation against evil portent

duas.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ طَيْرَ إِلاَّ طَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ خَيْرَ إِلاَّ خَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ)).
duas.translation_en: (This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk.)(O Allah, there is no omen but there is reliance on You, there is no good except Your good and none has the right to be worshipped except You.)
duas.reference: (This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk.)(Allahumma la tayra illa tayruk, wala khayra illa khayruk, wala ilaha ghayruk.)
duas.translation_fr: (Cette supplication est utilisée chaque fois que l'on pense initialement à un événement ou à un événement fortuit pour prédire le bien ou le mal, en l'utilisant comme base pour déterminer quelle action il doit entreprendre, mais il dénonce ensuite un tel lien, s'appuie sur Allah et dit ensuite cette supplication comme une expiation pour cet acte, puisqu'il entre dans la catégorie du shirk. (Ô Allah, il n'y a de présage que de confiance en Toi, il n'y a de bien que Ton bien et nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Toi.)
duas.phonetic: This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk.)(Allahumma la tayra illa tayruk, wala khayra illa khayruk, wala ilaha ghayruk.

title_en: Invocation for riding in a vehicle or on an animal

duas.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ للَّهِ ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾، ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي؛ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ)).
duas.translation_en: (In the name of Allah and all praise is for Allah.  How perfect He is, the One Who has placed this (transport) at our service and we ourselves would not have been capable of that, and to our Lord is our final destiny.  All praise is for Allah, All praise is for Allah, All praise is for Allah,  Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest. How perfect You are, O Allah, verily I have wronged my soul, so forgive me, for surely none can forgive sins except You.)
duas.reference: (Bismil-lah, walhamdu lillah, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wainna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanakal-lahumma innee thalamtu nafsee faghfir lee fainnahu la yaghfiruth-thunooba illa ant.)
duas.translation_fr: (Au nom d'Allah et toute louange est à Allah. Comme Il est parfait, Celui qui a mis ce (transport) à notre service et nous-mêmes n'en aurions pas été capables, et notre destinée finale est à notre Seigneur. Toute louange est à Allah, Toute louange est à Allah, Toute louange est à Allah, Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand. Comme tu es parfait, ô Allah, en vérité j'ai fait du tort à mon âme, alors pardonne-moi, car sûrement personne ne peut pardonner les péchés sauf Toi.)
duas.phonetic: Bismil-lah, walhamdu lillah, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wainna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanakal-lahumma innee thalamtu nafsee faghfir lee fainnahu la yaghfiruth-thunooba illa ant.

title_en: Invocation for traveling

duas.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ * وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾ ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِي سَفَرِنَا هَذَا البِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى، وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى، اللَّهُمَّ هَوِّنْ عَلَيْنَا سَفَرَنَا هَذَا وَاطْوِ عَنَّا بُعْدَهُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الصَّاحِبُ فِي السَّفَرِ، وَالْخَليفَةُ فِي الْأَهْلِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ وَعْثَاءِ السَّفَرِ، وَكَآبَةِ الْمَنْظَرِ، وَسُوءِ الْمُنْقَلَبِ فِي الْمَالِ وَالْأَهْلِ))، وإذا رَجَعَ قَالَهُنَّ وَزَادَ فِيهِنَّ: ((آيِبُونَ، تائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ)).
duas.translation_en: (Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, How perfect He is, The One Who has placed this (transport) at our service, and we ourselves would not have been capable of that, and to our Lord is our final destiny.  O Allah, we ask You for birr and taqwa in this journey of ours, and we ask You for deeds which please You.  O Allah, facilitate our journey and let us cover it’s distance quickly.  O Allah, You are The Companion on the journey and The Successor over the family, O Allah, I take refuge with You from the difficulties of travel, from having a change of hearts and being in a bad predicament, and I take refuge in You from an ill fated outcome with wealth and family.)(birr and taqwa: two comprehensive terms which individually, refer to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions.  When combined together, birr refers to doing those actions which have been commanded and taqwa refers to avoiding those actions which have been prohibited.)(A successor: one who succeeds another due to the latter’s absence or death.  This is the correct meaning of the word khaleefah; thus, it is incorrect to believe that Adam is the khaleefah (vicegerent, as is commonly translated) of Allah on earth because Allah is never absent, and will never die.  This supplication proves the correct understanding of this term and shows that Allah succeeds us and guards whom we leave behind when we die or are absent.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: (We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord.))
duas.reference: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wa-inna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, allahumma inna nas-aluka fee safarina hatha albirra wattaqwa, waminal-AAamali ma tarda, allahumma hawwin AAalayna safarana hatha, watwi AAanna buAAdah, allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safar, walkhaleefatu fil-ahl, allahumma innee aAAoothu bika min waAAtha-is-safar, waka-abatil-manthar, wasoo-il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahl.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: (Ayiboona, ta-iboona, AAabidoona, lirabbina hamidoon.))
duas.translation_fr: (Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand, Comme Il est parfait, Celui qui a mis ce (transport) à notre service, et nous-mêmes n'en aurions pas été capables, et notre destinée finale appartient à notre Seigneur. Ô Allah, nous Te demandons du birr et de la taqwa dans notre voyage, et nous Te demandons des actes qui Te plaisent. Ô Allah, facilite notre voyage et permettons-nous de parcourir la distance rapidement. Ô Allah, Tu es Notre Compagnon de voyage. et Le Successeur de la famille, ô Allah, je me réfugie auprès de Toi contre les difficultés du voyage, contre un changement d'avis et une mauvaise situation, et je me réfugie auprès de Toi contre une issue malheureuse avec la richesse et la famille.)(birr et taqwa : deux termes complets qui, individuellement, font référence à toutes les bonnes actions et à l'obéissance, c'est-à-dire accomplir les actions commandées et éviter les actions interdites. Lorsqu'ils sont combinés, birr fait référence à l'exécution des actions qui ont été commandées et taqwa fait référence à éviter ces actions qui ont été interdites.)(Un successeur : celui qui succède à un autre en raison de l'absence ou de la mort de ce dernier. C'est le sens correct du mot khaleefah ; ainsi, il est incorrect de croire qu'Adam est le khaleefah (vice-gérant, comme on le traduit communément) d'Allah sur terre parce qu'Allah n'est jamais absent et ne mourra jamais. Cette supplication prouve la compréhension correcte de ce terme et montre qu'Allah nous succède et garde ceux que nous laissons derrière nous lorsque nous mourons ou sommes absents.)(au retour du même la supplication est récitée avec l'ajout suivant : (Nous revenons, nous repentons, adorons et louons notre Seigneur.))
duas.phonetic: Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wa-inna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, allahumma inna nas-aluka fee safarina hatha albirra wattaqwa, waminal-AAamali ma tarda, allahumma hawwin AAalayna safarana hatha, watwi AAanna buAAdah, allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safar, walkhaleefatu fil-ahl, allahumma innee aAAoothu bika min waAAtha-is-safar, waka-abatil-manthar, wasoo-il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahl.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: (Ayiboona, ta-iboona, AAabidoona, lirabbina hamidoon.

title_en: Invocation for entering a town or city

duas.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَظْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الأَرَضِينَ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَقْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الشَّياطِينِ وَمَا أَضْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الرِّيَاحِ وَمَا ذَرَيْنَ، أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ، وَخَيْرَ أَهْلِهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ أَهْلِهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا)).
duas.translation_en: (O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and all that they envelop, Lord of the seven earths and all that they carry, Lord of the devils and all whom they misguide, Lord of the winds and all whom they whisk away. I ask You for the goodness of this village, the goodness of its inhabitants and for all the goodness found within it and I take refuge with You from the evil of this village, the evil of its inhabitants and from all the evil found within it.)
duas.reference: (Allahumma rabbas-samawatis-sabAAi wama athlaln, warabbal-aradeenas-sabAAi wama aqlaln, warabbash-shayateeni wama adlaln, warabbar-riyahi wama tharayn, as-aluka khayra hathihil-qaryah, wakhayra ahlilha wakhayra ma feeha, wa-aAAoothu bika min sharriha washarri ahliha, washarri ma feeha.)
duas.translation_fr: (Ô Allah, Seigneur des sept cieux et de tout ce qu'ils enveloppent, Seigneur des sept terres et de tout ce qu'ils transportent, Seigneur des diables et de tous ceux qu'ils égarent, Seigneur des vents et de tous ceux qu'ils emportent. Je Te demande la bonté de ce village, la bonté de ses habitants et pour toute la bonté qui s'y trouve et je me réfugie auprès de Toi contre le mal de ce village, le mal de ses habitants et contre tout le mal qui s'y trouve.)
duas.phonetic: Allahumma rabbas-samawatis-sabAAi wama athlaln, warabbal-aradeenas-sabAAi wama aqlaln, warabbash-shayateeni wama adlaln, warabbar-riyahi wama tharayn, as-aluka khayra hathihil-qaryah, wakhayra ahlilha wakhayra ma feeha, wa-aAAoothu bika min sharriha washarri ahliha, washarri ma feeha.

title_fr: Invocation en entrant dans une ville

title_en: Invocation for entering a market

duas.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ، وَهُوَ حَيٌّ لاِ يَمُوتُ، بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
duas.translation_en: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise.  He gives life and causes death, and He is living and does not die.  In His hand is all good and He is over all things, omnipotent.)
duas.reference: (La ilaha illal-lah,  wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, yuhyee wayumeetu wahuwa hayyun la yamoot, biyadihil-khayru wahuwa  AAala kulli shayin qadeer.)
duas.translation_fr: (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah, seul, sans associé, à Lui appartiennent toute la souveraineté et la louange. Il donne la vie et provoque la mort, et Il vit et ne meurt pas. Dans Sa main est tout bon et Il est au-dessus de toutes choses, Tout-Puissant.)
duas.phonetic: La ilaha illal-lah,  wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, yuhyee wayumeetu wahuwa hayyun la yamoot, biyadihil-khayru wahuwa  AAala kulli shayin qadeer.

title_en: Invocation for when your vehicle or mount begins to fail

duas.translation_en: (In the name of Allah.)
duas.reference: (Bismil-lah.)
duas.translation_fr: (Au nom d'Allah.)
duas.phonetic: Bismil-lah.

title_en: The traveler's invocation for the one he leaves behind

duas.arabic: ((أَسْتَوْدِعُكُمُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي لاَ تَضِيعُ وَدَائِعُهُ)).
duas.translation_en: (I place you in the trust of Allah, whose trust is never misplaced.)
duas.reference: (AstawdiAAukumul-lah, allathee la tadeeAAu wada-iAAuh.)
duas.translation_fr: (Je vous place dans la confiance d’Allah, dont la confiance n’est jamais mal placée.)
duas.phonetic: AstawdiAAukumul-lah, allathee la tadeeAAu wada-iAAuh.

title_en: The resident's invocations for the traveler

duas.arabic: (( أَسْتَوْدِعُ اللَّهَ دِينَكَ، وَأَمَانَتَكَ، وَخَوَاتِيمَ عَمَلِكَ)).
duas.translation_en: (I place your religion, your faithfulness and the ends of your deeds in the trust of Allah.)
duas.reference: (AstawdiAAul-laha deenak, wa-amanatak, wakhawateema AAamalik.)
duas.translation_fr: (Je place votre religion, votre fidélité et les fins de vos actes dans la confiance d'Allah.)
duas.phonetic: AstawdiAAul-laha deenak, wa-amanatak, wakhawateema AAamalik.

duas.arabic: ((زَوَّدَكَ اللَّهُ التَّقْوَى، وَغَفَرَ ذَنْبَكَ، وَيَسَّرَ لَكَ الخَيْرَ حَيْثُ ما كُنْتَ)).
duas.translation_en: (May Allah endow you with taqwa, forgive your sins and facilitate all good for you, wherever you be.)(taqwa: a comprehensive term which refers to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions.)
duas.reference: (Zawwadakal-lahut-taqwa, waghafara thanbak, wayassara lakal-khayra haythuma kunt.)
duas.translation_fr: (Qu'Allah vous accorde la taqwa, vous pardonne vos péchés et vous facilite tout le bien, où que vous soyez.)(taqwa : un terme complet qui fait référence à toutes les bonnes actions et à l'obéissance, c'est-à-dire accomplir les actions commandées et éviter les actions interdites.)
duas.phonetic: Zawwadakal-lahut-taqwa, waghafara thanbak, wayassara lakal-khayra haythuma kunt.

title_en: Glorifying and magnifying Allah on the journey

duas.arabic: قَالَ جَابِرٌ رضي الله عنه: ((كُنَّا إِذَا صَعَدْنَا كَبَّرْنَا، وَإِذَا نَزَلْنَا سَبَّحْنَا)).
duas.translation_en: Jabir said: Whenever we went up a hill we would say Allaahu 'Akber (Allah is the Most Great) and when we descended we would say Subhaanallaah (Glory is to Allah)
duas.translation_fr: Jabir a dit : Chaque fois que nous montions une colline, nous disions Allahu 'Akber (Allah est le plus grand) et lorsque nous descendions, nous disions Subhaanallah (Gloire à Allah).
duas.phonetic: qaala jaabirun rdy allh 'nh: ((kunaa iidhaa sa'adnaa kabarnaa waiidhaa nazalnaa sabahnaa)).

title_en: The traveler's invocation at dawn

duas.arabic: ((سَمَّعَ سَامِعٌ بِحَمْدِ اللَّهِ، وَحُسْنِ بَلاَئِهِ عَلَيْنَا، رَبَّنَا صاحِبْنَا، وَأَفْضِلْ عَلَيْنَا، عَائِذاً بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ)).
duas.translation_en: (May a witness, be witness to our praise of Allah for His favours and bounties upon us.  Our Lord, protect us, show favour on us and deliver us from every evil.  I take refuge in Allah from the fire.)
duas.reference: (SamiAAa samiAAun bihamdil-lahi wahusni bala-ihi AAalayna. Rabbana sahibna wa-afdil AAalayna AAa-ithan billahi minan-nar.)
duas.translation_fr: (Qu'un témoin, soit témoin de notre louange à Allah pour ses faveurs et ses bienfaits sur nous. Notre Seigneur, protège-nous, accorde-nous de la faveur et délivre-nous de tout mal. Je prends refuge auprès d'Allah contre le feu.)
duas.phonetic: SamiAAa samiAAun bihamdil-lahi wahusni bala-ihi AAalayna. Rabbana sahibna wa-afdil AAalayna AAa-ithan billahi minan-nar.

title_en: Invocation for a layover (stopping along the way) on the journey
title_ar: الدعاء إذا نزل منزلا في سفر أو غيره

duas.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ)).
duas.translation_en: (I take refuge in Allah’s perfect words from the evil that He has created.)
duas.reference: (aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq.)
duas.translation_fr: (Je me réfugie dans les paroles parfaites d’Allah contre le mal qu’Il ​​a créé.)
duas.phonetic: aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq.

title_fr: Invocation lors d'une halte en voyage

title_en: What to say upon returning from a Journey

duas.arabic: ((يُكَبِّرُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَرَفٍ ثَلاَثَ تَكْبِيرَاتٍ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنا حَامِدُونَ، صَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزابَ وَحْدَهُ)).
duas.translation_en: (Ibn AAumar  reported that the Messenger of Allah  on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: (Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest.) and then he would say: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner.  To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise, and He is over all things omnipotent.  We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord.  Allah fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant, and single-handedly defeated the allies.))
duas.reference: (Ibn AAumar  reported that the Messenger of Allah  on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shay-in qadeer, ayiboona ta-iboon, AAabidoon, lirabbina hamidoon, sadaqal-lahu waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal-ahzaba wahdah.))
duas.translation_fr: (Ibn AAumar a rapporté que le Messager d'Allah, au retour d'une bataille ou d'un pèlerinage, disait à chaque moment fort : (Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand.) puis il disait : (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah, seul, sans associé. À Lui appartiennent toute la souveraineté et la louange, et Il est sur toutes choses tout-puissant. Nous revenons, nous repentons, adorons et louons notre Seigneur. Allah a accompli Sa promesse, a aidé Son Serviteur et à lui seul. vaincu les alliés.))
duas.phonetic: Ibn AAumar  reported that the Messenger of Allah  on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shay-in qadeer, ayiboona ta-iboon, AAabidoon, lirabbina hamidoon, sadaqal-lahu waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal-ahzaba wahdah.

title_en: What to say if something happens to please you or to displease you
title_ar: ما يقول ويفعل من أتاه أمر يسره أو يكرهه

duas.arabic: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَسُرُّهُ قَالَ: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ)) وَإِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَكْرَهُهُ قَالَ: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ)).
duas.translation_en: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet used to say:((Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat)). Praise is to Allah Who by His blessings all good things are perfected. And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say:((Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal)).Praise is to Allah in all circumstances.
duas.reference: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet used to say:((Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat)).And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say:((Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal)).
duas.translation_fr: Lorsqu'il arrivait quelque chose qui lui plaisait, le Prophète avait l'habitude de dire : ((Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat)). La louange est à Allah qui, par Ses bénédictions, rend parfaites toutes les bonnes choses. Et s'il arrivait quelque chose qui lui déplaisait, il avait l'habitude de dire : ((Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal)). La louange est à Allah en toutes circonstances.
duas.phonetic: kaana alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm iidhaa aataahu alaamru yasuruhu qaala: ((alhamdu lilahi aladhiy bini'matihi tatimu alsaalihaatu)) waiidhaa aataahu alaamru yakrahuhu qaala: ((alhamdu lilahi 'alaa kuli haalin)).

title_fr: Que dire quand quelque chose vous plait ou deplait

title_en: The excellence of asking for Allah's blessings upon the Prophet pbuh
title_ar: فضل الصلاة على النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم

duas.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيَّ صَلاَةً صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ بِهَا عَشْراً)).
duas.translation_en: (The Prophet (PBUH) said: ‘Whoever sends a prayer upon me, Allah sends ten upon him.)
duas.translation_fr: (Le Prophète (PSL) a dit : « Quiconque envoie une prière sur moi, Allah en envoie dix sur lui.)
duas.phonetic: qaala alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((man salaa 'alaya salaahan salaa allahu 'alayhi bihaa 'ashraan)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لاَ تَجْعَلُوا قَبْرِي عِيداً وَصَلُّوا عَلَيَّ؛ فَإِنَّ صَلاَتَكُم تَبْلُغُنِي حَيْثُ كُنْتُمْ)).
duas.translation_en: (He (PBUH) also said: ‘Do not take my grave as a place of habitual ceremony.  Send prayers upon me, for verily your prayers reach me wherever you are.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il (PSL) a également dit : « Ne prenez pas ma tombe comme lieu de cérémonie habituelle. Envoyez-moi des prières, car en vérité vos prières me parviennent où que vous soyez.)
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((laa taj'aluwa qabriy 'iydaan wasaluwa 'alaya faiina salaatakum tablughuniy haythu kuntum)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((الْبَخِيلُ مَنْ ذُكِرْتُ عِنْدَهُ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ عَلَيَّ)).
duas.translation_en: (He (PBUH) also said: ‘A miser is one whom when I am mentioned to him,  fails to send prayers upon me.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il (PSL) a également dit : « Un avare est celui qui, lorsqu'on lui parle, ne parvient pas à m'envoyer des prières.)
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((albakhiylu man dhukirtu 'indahu falam yusali 'alaya)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم:((إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلاَئِكَةً سَيَّاحِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يُبَلِّغُونِي مِنْ أُمَّتِي السَّلاَمَ)).
duas.translation_en: The Prophet [PBUH] said:'Indeed Allah has angels who roam the earth and they convey to me the greetings (or prayers of peace) of my 'Ummah (nation).
duas.translation_fr: Le Prophète (PSL) a dit : « En effet, Allah a des anges qui parcourent la terre et ils me transmettent les salutations (ou prières de paix) de ma 'Umma (nation).
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm:((iina lilahi malaaikahan sayaahiyna fiy alaardi yubalighuwniy min aumatiy alsalaama)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَيَّ إِلاَّ رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ رُوحِيَ حَتَّى أَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمَ)).
duas.translation_en: The Prophet [PBUH] said: 'No one sends greetings (or prayers of peace) upon me but Allah returns my soul to me so that I may return his greetings
duas.translation_fr: Le Prophète (PSL) a dit : « Personne ne m'envoie des salutations (ou des prières de paix) mais Allah me rend mon âme afin que je puisse lui rendre ses salutations. »
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((maa min aahadin yusalimu 'alaya iilaa rada allahu 'alaya ruwhiya hataa aaruda 'alayhi alsalaama)).

title_en: spreading the greetings of Salam (Peace)

duas.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لاَ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى تُؤْمِنُوا، وَلاَ تُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّى تَحَابُّوا، أَوَلاَ أَدُلُّكُم عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِذَا فَعَلْتُمُوهُ تَحَابَبْتُم، أَفْشُوا السَّلاَمَ بَيْنَكُمْ)).
duas.translation_en: (The Messenger of Allah  said: ‘You shall not enter paradise until you believe, and you shall not believe until you love one another. Shall I not inform you of something, if you were to act upon it, you will indeed achieve mutual love for one another?  Spread the greeting amongst yourselves.)
duas.translation_fr: (Le Messager d’Allah a dit : « Vous n’entrerez pas au paradis tant que vous n’aurez pas cru, et vous ne croirez pas tant que vous ne vous aimerez pas les uns les autres. Ne dois-je pas vous informer de quelque chose, si vous agissez en conséquence, vous parviendrez effectivement à l’amour mutuel ? Répandez le salut entre vous.)
duas.phonetic: qaala rasuwlu allahi sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((laa tadkhuluwa aljanaha hataa tuminuwa walaa tuminuwa hataa tahaabuwa aawalaa aadulukum 'alaa shay'in iidhaa fa'altumuwhu tahaababtum aafshuwa alsalaama baynakum)).

duas.arabic: ((ثَلاَثٌ مَنْ جَمَعَهُنَّ فَقَدْ جَمَعَ الْإِيمَانَ: الْإِنْصَافُ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ، وَبَذْلُ السَّلاَمِ لِلْعَالَمِ، وَالْإِنْفَاقُ مِنَ الإِقْتَارِ)).
duas.translation_en: (Aammar  said: ‘Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has completed his faith: to be just, to spread greetings to all people and to spend (charitably) out of the little you have.)
duas.translation_fr: (Aammar a dit : « Trois caractéristiques, quiconque les combine, a complété sa foi : être juste, répandre les salutations à tous et dépenser (charitablement) le peu que vous avez.)
duas.phonetic: ((thalaathun man jama'ahuna faqad jama'a aliiymaana: aliinsaafu min nafsika wabadhlu alsalaami lil'aalami waaliinfaaqu mina aliiqtaari)).

duas.arabic: وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: أنَّ رَجُلاً سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أيُّ الْإِسْلاَمِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ: ((تُطْعِمُ الطَّعَامَ، وَتَقْرأُ السَّلاَمَ عَلَى مَنْ عَرَفْتَ وَمَنْ لَمْ تَعْرِفْ)).
duas.translation_en: (Aabdullah Ibn Aamr  reported that a man asked the Prophet : ‘Which Islam is the best?’.  He  replied: Feed (the poor), and greet those whom you know as well as those whom you do not.)
duas.translation_fr: (Aabdullah Ibn Aamr a rapporté qu'un homme a demandé au Prophète : « Quel Islam est le meilleur ? ». Il a répondu : Nourrissez (les pauvres) et saluez ceux que vous connaissez ainsi que ceux que vous ne connaissez pas.)
duas.phonetic: wa'an 'abdi allahi bni 'umara radiya allahu 'anhumaa: ana rajulaan saaala alnabiya sla allh 'lyh wslm ayu aliislaami khayrun qaala: ((tut'imu alta'aama wataqrau alsalaama 'alaa man 'arafta waman lam ta'rif)).

title_en: How to reply to a disbeliever if he says Salam to you
title_ar: كيف يرد السلام على الكافر إذا سلم

duas.arabic: ((إذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ فَقُولُوا: وَعَلَيْكُمْ)).
duas.translation_en: (When the people of the Book greet you, reply by saying: (And upon you.))
duas.translation_fr: (Quand les gens du Livre vous saluent, répondez en disant : (Et sur vous.))
duas.phonetic: ((idhaa salama 'alaykum aahlu alkitaabi faquwluwa: wa'alaykum)).

title_en: Invocation upon hearing the cock's crow or the bray of a donkey

duas.arabic: ((إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ صِيَاحَ الدِّيَكَةِ فَاسْأَلُوا اللَّهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ؛ فَإِنَّهَا رَأَتْ مَلَكاً وَإِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نَهِيقَ الْحِمَارِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ؛ فَإِنَّهُ رَأَى شَيْطَاناً)).
duas.translation_en: (If you hear the crow of a rooster, ask Allah for his bounty for it has seen an angel and if you hear the braying of an ass, seek refuge in Allah for it has seen a devil.)
duas.translation_fr: (Si vous entendez le chant d'un coq, demandez à Allah sa grâce car il a vu un ange et si vous entendez le braiment d'un âne, cherchez refuge auprès d'Allah car il a vu un diable.)
duas.phonetic: ((iidhaa sami'tum siyaaha aldiyakahi faasaaluwa allaha min fadlihi faiinahaa raaat malakaan waiidhaa sami'tum nahiyqa alhimaari fata'awadhuwa biallahi mina alshaytaani faiinahu raaaa shaytaanaan)).

title_en: Invocation upon hearing a dog barking in the night

duas.arabic: ((إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نُبَاحَ الْكِلاَبِ وَنَهِيقَ الْحَمِيرِ بِاللَّيْلِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُنَّ؛ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يَرَيْنَ مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ)).
duas.translation_en: (If you hear the barking of dogs or the braying of asses at night, seek refuge in Allah for they see what you do not.)
duas.translation_fr: (Si vous entendez des aboiements de chiens ou des braiments d'ânes la nuit, cherchez refuge auprès d'Allah car ils voient ce que vous ne voyez pas.)
duas.phonetic: ((iidhaa sami'tum nubaaha alkilaabi wanahiyqa alhamiyri biallayli fata'awadhuwa biallahi minhuna faiinahuna yarayna maa laa tarawna)).

title_en: Invocation for someone you have spoken ill to

duas.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((اللَّهُمَّ فَأَيُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ سَبَبْتُهُ فَاجْعَلْ ذَلِكَ لَهُ قُرْبَةً إِلَيْكَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ)).
duas.translation_en: (O Allah, to any believer whom I have insulted, let that be cause to draw him near to You on the Day of Resurrection.)
duas.reference: (Allahumma fa-ayyuma mu/minin sababtuhu fajAAal thalika lahu qurbatan ilayka yawmal-qiyamah.)
duas.translation_fr: (Ô Allah, que tout croyant que j'ai insulté soit une raison pour l'attirer près de Toi le Jour de la Résurrection.)
duas.phonetic: Allahumma fa-ayyuma mu/minin sababtuhu fajAAal thalika lahu qurbatan ilayka yawmal-qiyamah.

title_fr: Invocation pour celui dont on a medit

title_en: How a Muslim should praise another Muslim
title_ar: ما يقول المسلم إذا مدح المسلم

duas.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُم مَادِحاً صَاحِبَهُ لاَ مَحَالَةَ فَلْيَقُلْ: أَحْسِبُ فُلاَناً وَاللَّهُ حَسِيبُهُ، وَلاَ أُزَكِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ أَحَداً، أَحْسِبُهُ – إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ – كَذَا وَكَذَا)).
duas.translation_en: (He  said: ‘If anyone of you is impelled to praise his brother, then he should say: ‘I deem so-and-so to be…and Allah is his reckoner…and I don’t praise anyone, putting it (i.e. my praising) forward, in front of Allah’s commendation, however I assume him so and so’…if he knows that of him.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il a dit : « Si quelqu’un d’entre vous est poussé à louer son frère, alors il devrait dire : « Je considère qu’un tel est… et Allah est son juge… et je ne loue personne, en le mettant (c’est-à-dire mes louanges) devant l’éloge d’Allah, quelle que soit la manière dont je l’assume tel et tel »… s’il sait cela de lui.)
duas.phonetic: qaala alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((iidhaa kaana aahadukum maadihaan saahibahu laa mahaalaha falyaqul: aahsibu fulaanaan waallahu hasiybuhu walaa auzakiy 'alaa allahi aahadaan aahsibuhu iin kaana ya'lamu dhaaka kadhaa wakadhaa)).

title_en: What a Muslim should say when he is praised

duas.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ، [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْراً مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ])).
duas.translation_en: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine].
duas.reference: Allaahumma laa tu'aakhithnee bimaa yaqooloona, waghfir lee maa laa ya'lamoona [waj'alnee khayram-mimmaa yadhunnoon]
duas.translation_fr: Ô Allah, ne me demande pas compte de ce qu'ils disent et pardonne-moi ce dont ils n'ont aucune connaissance [et rends-moi meilleur qu'ils ne l'imaginent].
duas.phonetic: ((allahuma laa tuaakhidhniy bimaa yaquwluwna waaghfir liy maa laa ya'lamuwna [waaj'alniy khayraan mimaa yadhunuwna])).

title_en: The pilgrim's announcement of his arrival for Hajj or Umrah
title_ar: كيف يلبي المحرم في الحج أو العمرة

duas.arabic: ((لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ، وَالنِّعْمَةَ، لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ)).
duas.translation_en: (Here I am O Allah, (in response to Your call), here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am.  Verily all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.)
duas.reference: (Labbaykal-lahumma labbayk, labbayka la shareeka laka labbayk, innal-hamda wanniAAmata laka walmulk, la shareeka lak.)
duas.translation_fr: (Me voici, Ô Allah, (en réponse à Ton appel), me voici. Me voici, Tu n'as pas de partenaire, me voici. En vérité, toute louange, grâce et souveraineté t'appartiennent. Tu n'as pas de partenaire.)
duas.phonetic: Labbaykal-lahumma labbayk, labbayka la shareeka laka labbayk, innal-hamda wanniAAmata laka walmulk, la shareeka lak.

title_en: Saying Allahu Akbar when passing the Black Stone

duas.arabic: ((طَافَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ كُلَّمَا أَتَى الرُّكْنَ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ بِشَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ وَكَبَّرَ)).
duas.translation_en: (The Prophet  circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: (Allah is the greatest))
duas.reference: (The Prophet  circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: (Allahu akbar))
duas.translation_fr: (Le Prophète faisait le tour de la KaAAbah sur un chameau, chaque fois qu'il atteignait la pierre noire, il la montrait avec son bâton et disait : (Allah est le plus grand))
duas.phonetic: The Prophet  circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: (Allahu akbar

title_en: Invocation to be recited between the Yemenite Corner and the Black Stone
title_ar: الدعاء بين الركن اليماني والحجر الأسود

duas.arabic: ((﴿رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ﴾)).
duas.translation_en: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the Yemeni corner and the black stone: (O our Lord, grant us the best in this life and the best in the next life, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.)
duas.reference: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the Yemeni corner and the black stone: (Rabbana atina fee alddunya hasanatan wafee al-akhirati hasanatan waqina  AAathaba alnnar)[Al-Baqarah: 201]
duas.translation_fr: Le Prophète PSL disait entre le coin yéménite et la pierre noire : (Ô notre Seigneur, accorde-nous le meilleur dans cette vie et le meilleur dans l'autre vie, et protège-nous du châtiment du Feu.)
duas.phonetic: ((﴿rabanaa aatinaa fiy aldunyaa hasanahan wafiy alaakhirahi hasanahan waqinaa 'adhaaba alnaari﴾)).

title_fr: Invocation entre le coin yemenite et la pierre noire

title_en: Invocation to be recited while standing at Safa and Marwah

duas.arabic: لَمَّا دَنَا النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الصَّفَا قَرَأَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَآئِرِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ)) فَبَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا فَرَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْبَيْتَ، فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ، فَوَحَّدَ اللَّهَ وَكبَّرَهُ وَقَالَ: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، أَنْجَزَ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ، ثُمَّ دَعَا بَيْنَ ذلكَ. قَالَ مِثْلَ هَذَا ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ)) الْحَدِيثُ. وَفِيهِ: ((فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا)).
duas.translation_en: (Jabir  said when describing the Prophet’s  pilgrimage: ‘…and when he approached mount Safa he recited: (Indeed Safa and Marwah are from the places of worship of Allah…) (I begin with what Allah began with.) so he started with Safa and climed it until he could see the KaAAbah, he then faced it and said: (Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest.) and then he would say the following three times making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication) after each time: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner.To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things amnipotent. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah alone.  He fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant and single-handedly defeated the allies.)) (he  would repeat this action at Marwah.)
duas.reference: (Jabir  said when describing the Prophet’s  pilgrimage: ‘…and when he approached mount Safa he recited: ( Innas-safa wa-almarwata min shaAAa-iri Allah ….) (Abda-o bima badaal-lahu bih.) so he started with Safa and climed it until he could see the KaAAbah, he then faced it and said: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say the following three times making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication) after each time: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer, la ilaha illal-lahu wahdah, anjaza waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal -ahzaba wahdah.)(he  would repeat this action at Marwah.)
duas.translation_fr: (Jabir a dit en décrivant le pèlerinage du Prophète : « … et quand il s'est approché du mont Safa, il a récité : (En effet Safa et Marwah sont des lieux de culte d'Allah…) (Je commence par ce par quoi Allah a commencé.) alors il a commencé par Safa et l'a gravi jusqu'à ce qu'il puisse voir la KaAAbah, il lui a ensuite fait face et a dit : (Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand, Allah est le plus grand.) et ensuite il disait les trois fois suivantes en faisant une supplication (il faut faire une supplication personnelle) après chaque fois : (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah, seul, sans partenaire. À Lui appartiennent toute souveraineté et louange et Il est sur toutes choses tout-puissant. Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah seul. Il a rempli sa promesse, a aidé son serviteur et a vaincu à lui seul les alliés.)) (il répéterait cette action à Marwah.)
duas.phonetic: Jabir  said when describing the Prophet’s  pilgrimage: ‘…and when he approached mount Safa he recited: ( Innas-safa wa-almarwata min shaAAa-iri Allah ….) (Abda-o bima badaal-lahu bih.) so he started with Safa and climed it until he could see the KaAAbah, he then faced it and said: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say the following three times making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication) after each time: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer, la ilaha illal-lahu wahdah, anjaza waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal -ahzaba wahdah.)(he  would repeat this action at Marwah.

title_en: Invocation to be recited on the Day of Arafat

duas.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((خَيْرُ الدُّعَاءِ دُعَاءُ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ، وَخَيْرُ مَا قُلْتُ أَنَا وَالنَّبيُّونَ مِنْ قَبْلِي: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
duas.translation_en: (The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all praise and sovereignty and He is over all things omnipotent.))
duas.reference: (The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer.))
duas.translation_fr: (La meilleure des supplications est celle du jour de AAarafah et la meilleure que moi et les prophètes avant moi avons dit (est) : (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah, seul, sans associé. À Lui appartiennent toute louange et souveraineté et Il est sur toutes choses tout-puissant.))
duas.phonetic: The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer.

title_en: Supplication to be recited at the sacred area of Muzdalifah

duas.arabic: رَكِبَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ (فَدَعَاهُ، وَكَبَّرَهُ، وَهَللَّهُ، وَوَحَّدَهُ) فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفاً حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدَّاً فَدَفَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمسُ.
duas.translation_en: The Prophet rode his camel, Al-Qaswa, until he reached the sacred area (Al-Mash aril Haraam). Then he faced the Qiblah and invoked Allah, and repeatedly said the words: Allaahu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great), Allaahu Ahad (Allah is One) and Laa ilaaha illallaah (There is none worthy of worship but Allah). He remained stationary until the sky became yellow with the dawn, and then pressed on before sunrise.
duas.translation_fr: Le Prophète montait sur son chameau, Al-Qaswa, jusqu'à ce qu'il atteigne la zone sacrée (Al-Mash aril Haraam). Puis il fit face à la Qiblah et invoqua Allah, et répéta à plusieurs reprises les mots : Allahu Akbar (Allah est le plus grand), Allahu Ahad (Allah est Un) et Laa ilaaha illallahah (Il n'y a personne digne d'adoration à part Allah). Il resta immobile jusqu'à ce que le ciel devienne jaune à l'aube, puis poursuivit son chemin avant le lever du soleil.
duas.phonetic: rakiba alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm alqaswaa'a hataa aataa almash'ara alharaama faastaqbala alqiblaha (fada'aahu wakabarahu wahallahu wawahadahu) falam yazal waaqifaan hataa aasfara jidaaan fadafa'a qabla aan tatlu'a alshamsu.

title_en: Saying Allahu Akbar while stoning the three pillars at Mina
title_ar: التكبيرة عند رمي الجمار مع كل حصاة

duas.arabic: ((يُكَبِّرُ كُلَّمَا رَمَى بِحَصَاةٍ عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ الثَّلاَثِ، ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمُ، ويَقِفُ يَدْعُو مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبلَةِ، رَافِعاً يَدَيْهِ بَعْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ. أَمَّا جَمْرَةُ الْعَقَبَةِ فَيَرْمِيهَا وَيُكَبِّرُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ وَيَنْصَرِفُ وَلاَ يَقِفُ عِنْدَهَا)).
duas.translation_en: The Prophet PBUH said Allaahu 'Akbar (Allah is the Most Great) with each pebble he threw at the three pillars. Then he went forward, stood facing the Qiblah and raised his hands and called upon Allah. That was after (stoning) the first and second pillar As for the third, he stoned it and called out Allahu Akbar with every pebble he threw but when he was finished he left without standing at it (for supplications).
duas.translation_fr: Le Prophète PBSL a dit Allahu 'Akbar (Allah est le Plus Grand) avec chaque caillou qu'il lançait sur les trois piliers. Puis il s'avança, se plaça face à la Qiblah, leva les mains et invoqua Allah. C'était après (lapidation) le premier et le deuxième pilier. Quant au troisième, il l'a lapidé et a appelé Allahu Akbar avec chaque caillou qu'il a lancé, mais quand il a eu fini, il est parti sans se tenir dessus (pour les supplications).
duas.phonetic: ((yukabiru kulamaa ramaa bihasaahin 'inda aljimaari althalaathi thuma yataqadamu wyaqifu yad'uw mustaqbila alqiblahi raafi'aan yadayhi ba'da aljamrahi alauwlaa waalthaaniyahi. aamaa jamrahu al'aqabahi fayarmiyhaa wayukabiru 'inda kuli hasaahin wayansarifu walaa yaqifu 'indahaa)).

title_fr: Le takbir lors de la lapidation a Mina

title_en: What to say when surprised or startled
title_ar: ما يقول عند التعجب والأمر السار

duas.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ!)).
duas.translation_en: (How perfect Allah is.)
duas.reference: (Subhanal-lah!.)
duas.translation_fr: (Comme Allah est parfait.)
duas.phonetic: Subhanal-lah!.

duas.translation_en: (Allah is the greatest.)
duas.reference: (Allahu akbar.)
duas.translation_fr: (Allah est le plus grand.)
duas.phonetic: Allahu akbar.

title_fr: Que dire en cas de frayeur

title_en: What to say when something that pleases you happens
title_ar: ما يفعل من أتاه أمر يسره

duas.arabic: ((كَانَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ أَمْرٌ يَسُرُّهُ أَوْ يُسَرُّ بِهِ خَرَّ سَاجِداً شُكْراً لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى)).
duas.translation_en: (The Prophet PBUH would prostrate in gratitude to Allah upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure. )
duas.translation_fr: (Le Prophète (PSL) se prosternait en signe de gratitude envers Allah lorsqu'il recevait des nouvelles qui lui plaisaient ou lui causaient du plaisir.)
duas.phonetic: ((kaana alnabyu sla allh 'lyh wslm iidhaa aataahu aamrun yasuruhu aaw yusaru bihi khara saajidaan shukraan lilahi tabaaraka wata'aalaa)).

title_en: What to say when you feel a pain in your body
title_ar: ما يقول من أحس وجعاً في جسده

duas.arabic: ((ضَعْ يَدَكَ عَلَى الَّذِي تَألَّمَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ وَقُلْ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، ثَلاَثاً، وَقُلْ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ)).
duas.translation_en: (Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: (In the name of Allah(three times)) the supplicate seven times: (I take refuge in Allah and within His omnipotence from the evil that I feel and am wary of.))
duas.reference: (Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: (Bismil-lah (three times)) the supplicate seven times: (aAAoothu billahi waqudratih min sharri ma ajidu wa-ohathir.))
duas.translation_fr: (Placez votre main sur le site de la douleur et dites : (Au nom d'Allah (trois fois)) invoquez sept fois : (Je me réfugie en Allah et au sein de Sa toute-puissance contre le mal que je ressens et dont je me méfie.))
duas.phonetic: Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: (Bismil-lah (three times)) the supplicate seven times: (aAAoothu billahi waqudratih min sharri ma ajidu wa-ohathir.

title_en: What to say when you fear you may afflict something with the evil eye
title_ar: دعاء من خشي أن يصيب شيئاً بعينه

duas.arabic: ((إِذَا رَأَى أَحَدُكُم مِنْ أَخِيهِ، أَوْ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ، أَوْ مِنْ مَالِهِ مَا يُعْجِبُهُ [فَلْيَدْعُ لَهُ بِالْبَرَكَةِ] فَإِنَّ الْعَيْنَ حَقٌّ)).
duas.translation_en: If you see anything of your brother that pleases you, or of his person or of his property [then ask Allah to bless him in it] for the envious eye is real.
duas.translation_fr: Si vous voyez quelque chose de votre frère qui vous plaît, ou de sa personne ou de ses biens, alors demandez à Allah de le bénir, car l'œil envieux est réel.
duas.phonetic: ((iidhaa raaaa aahadukum min aakhiyhi aaw min nafsihi aaw min maalihi maa yu'jibuhu [falyad'u lahu bialbarakahi] faiina al'ayna haqun)).

title_en: What to say when you feel frightened

duas.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ!)).
duas.translation_en: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah.)
duas.reference: (La ilaha illal-lah.)
duas.translation_fr: (Nul n’a le droit d’être adoré sauf Allah.)
duas.phonetic: La ilaha illal-lah.

title_fr: Que dire quand on a peur

title_en: What to say when slaughtering or sacrificing an animal
title_ar: ما يقول عند الذبح أو النحر

duas.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ [اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ] اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي)).
duas.translation_en: (In the name of Allah, and Allah is the greatest.  O Allah, (it is) from You and belongs to You, O Allah, accept this from me.)
duas.reference: (Bismil-lah wallahu akbar, allahumma minka walak, allahumma taqabbal  minnee.)
duas.translation_fr: (Au nom d'Allah, et Allah est le plus grand. Ô Allah, (cela vient) de Toi et t'appartient, ô Allah, accepte ceci de ma part.)
duas.phonetic: Bismil-lah wallahu akbar, allahumma minka walak, allahumma taqabbal  minnee.

title_fr: Que dire lors de l'immolation d'une bete

title_en: What to say to foil the devil's plots
title_ar: ما يقول لرد كيد مردة الشياطين

duas.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ الَّتِي لاَ يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ وَلاَ فَاجِرٌ: مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ، وَبَرَأَ وَذَرَأَ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَعْرُجُ فيهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا ذَرَأَ فِي الْأَرْضِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتَنِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ طَارِقٍ إِلاَّ طَارِقاً يَطْرُقُ بِخَيْرٍ يَا رَحْمَنُ)).
duas.translation_en: (I take refuge within Allah’s perfect words which no righteous or unrighteous person can transgress, from all the evil that He has created, made and originated.  (I take refuge) from the evil that descends from the sky and the evil that rises up to it.  (I take refuge) from the evil that is spread on Earth and the evil that springs from her, and I take refuge from the evil of the tribulations of night and day, and the evil of one who visits at night except the one who brings good, O Merciful One.)
duas.reference: (aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammat, allatee la yujawizuhunna barrun wala fajir min sharri ma khalaq, wabaraa watharaa, wamin sharri ma yanzilu minas-sama/, wamin sharri ma yaAAruju feeha, wamin sharri ma tharaa fil-ard, wamin sharri ma yakhruju minha, wamin sharri fitnanil-layli wannahar, wamin sharri kulli tariqin illa tariqan yatruqu bikhayrin ya Rahman.)
duas.translation_fr: (Je me réfugie dans les paroles parfaites d'Allah, qu'aucun juste ou injuste ne peut transgresser, contre tout le mal qu'Il a créé, fait et originaire. (Je me réfugie) contre le mal qui descend du ciel et le mal qui monte vers lui. (Je me réfugie) contre le mal qui se répand sur la terre et le mal qui en jaillit, et je me réfugie contre le mal des tribulations de la nuit et du jour, et du mal de celui qui visite la nuit sauf celui qui apporte le bien, Ô Miséricordieux.)
duas.phonetic: aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammat, allatee la yujawizuhunna barrun wala fajir min sharri ma khalaq, wabaraa watharaa, wamin sharri ma yanzilu minas-sama/, wamin sharri ma yaAAruju feeha, wamin sharri ma tharaa fil-ard, wamin sharri ma yakhruju minha, wamin sharri fitnanil-layli wannahar, wamin sharri kulli tariqin illa tariqan yatruqu bikhayrin ya Rahman.

title_en: Repentance and seeking forgiveness

duas.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لأَسْتَغفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ فِي الْيَوْمِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةٍ)).
duas.translation_en: (The Messenger of Allah  said: ‘By Allah, I seek forgiveness and repent to Allah, more than seventy times a day.)
duas.translation_fr: (Le Messager d’Allah a dit : « Par Allah, je demande pardon et je me repens à Allah plus de soixante-dix fois par jour.)
duas.phonetic: qaala rasuwlu allahi sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((waallahi iiniy laastaghfiru allaha waaatuwbu iilayhi fiy alyawmi aakthara min sab'iyna marahin)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَإِنِّي أَتُوبُ فِي الْيَوْمِ إِلَيْهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
duas.translation_en: (He  also said: ‘O People, Repent! Verily I repent to Allah, a hundred times a day.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il a également dit : « Ô gens, repentez-vous ! En vérité, je me repens à Allah cent fois par jour.)
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((yaa aayuhaa alnaasu tuwbuwa iilaa allahi faiiniy aatuwbu fiy alyawmi iilayhi miaaha marahin)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ قَالَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ، غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَإِنْ كَانَ فَرَّ مِنَ الزَّحْفِ)).
duas.translation_en: (He  also said: ‘Whoever says:(I seek Allah’s forgiveness, besides whom, none has the right to be worshipped except He, The Ever Living, The Self-Subsisting and Supporter of all, and I turn to Him in repentance.) Allah would forgive him even if he was one who fled during the advance of an army.)
duas.reference: (He  also said: ‘Whoever says:(Astaghfirul-lahal-lathee la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyoomu wa-atoobu ilayh.) Allah would forgive him even if he was one who fled during the advance of an army.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il a également dit : « Celui qui dit : (Je demande le pardon d’Allah, en dehors de Lui, nul n’a le droit d’être adoré sauf Lui, Celui qui est éternel, Celui qui subsiste par lui-même et qui soutient tous, et je me tourne vers Lui dans la repentance.) Allah lui pardonnerait même s’il était quelqu’un qui s’enfuyait lors de l’avancée d’une armée.)
duas.phonetic: He  also said: ‘Whoever says:(Astaghfirul-lahal-lathee la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyoomu wa-atoobu ilayh.) Allah would forgive him even if he was one who fled during the advance of an army.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الرَّبُّ مِنَ الْعَبْدِ فِي جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ الآخِرِ فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ فِي تِلْكَ السَّاعَةِ فَكُنْ)).
duas.translation_en: (He  said: ‘The nearest the Lord comes to His servant is in the middle of the night, so if you are able to be of those who remember Allah at that time, then be so.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il dit : « Le Seigneur se rapproche le plus de Son serviteur au milieu de la nuit, donc si vous pouvez être de ceux qui se souviennent d’Allah à ce moment-là, alors soyez-le.)
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((aaqrabu maa yakuwnu alrabu mina al'abdi fiy jawfi allayli alaakhiri faiini astata'ta aan takuwna miman yadhkuru allaha fiy tilka alsaa'ahi fakun)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الْعَبْدُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَهُوَ سَاجِدٌ فَأَكثِرُوا الدُّعَاءَ)).
duas.translation_en: (He  also said: ‘The nearest a servant is to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so supplicate much therein.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il a également dit : « Le serviteur est le plus proche de son Seigneur lorsqu’il se prosterne, alors invoquez beaucoup.)
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((aaqrabu maa yakuwnu al'abdu min rabihi wahuwa saajidun faaakthiruwa aldu'aa'a)).

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِنَّهُ لَيُغَانُ عَلَى قَلْبِي وَإِنِّي لأَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ فِي الْيَوْمِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
duas.translation_en: (He  also said: ‘verily my heart becomes preoccupied, and verily I seek Allah’s forgiveness a hundred times a day.)(preoccupied: i.e. in a state of ‘forgetfulness’. The Prophet  always used to increase in his remembrance of his Lord, in attaining a nearness to Allah and having consciousness of Allah to the extent that if this intensity lessened in anyway, he would regard it as a sin and would then race to seek forgiveness from Allah.)
duas.translation_fr: (Il a également dit : « En vérité, mon cœur devient préoccupé et en vérité je demande le pardon d’Allah cent fois par jour.) (préoccupé : c’est-à-dire dans un état d’« oubli ». Le Prophète avait toujours l’habitude d’augmenter son souvenir de son Seigneur, en atteignant une proximité avec Allah et en ayant la conscience d’Allah dans la mesure où si cette intensité diminuait de toute façon, il le considérerait comme un péché et se précipiterait alors pour demander pardon à Allah.)
duas.phonetic: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: ((iinahu layughaanu 'alaa qalbiy waiiniy laastaghfiru allaha fiy alyawmi miaaha marahin)).

title_fr: Le repentir et la demande de pardon

title_en: The excellence of remembering Allah

duas.arabic: قَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ فِي يَوْمٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ حُطَّتْ خَطَايَاهُ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ مِثْلَ زَبَدِ الْبَحْر)).
duas.translation_en: (Whoever says:(How perfect Allah is and I praise Him.) a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea.)
duas.reference: (Whoever says:(Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih.) a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea.)
duas.translation_fr: (Quiconque dit : (Comme Allah est parfait et je Le loue.) cent fois par jour, ses péchés sont effacés, même s'ils sont comme l'écume de la mer.)
duas.phonetic: Whoever says:(Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih.) a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ قَالَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ عَشْرَ مِرَارٍ، كَانَ كَمَنْ أَعْتَقَ أَرْبَعَةَ أَنْفُسٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ)).
duas.translation_en: (Abu Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Whoever says: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things omnipotent.) a hundred times during the day, has the reward of freeing ten slaves, a hundred good deeds are recorded for him and a hundred bad deeds are wiped away and he has gained refuge from the devil that day until evening and none shall come with anything better except someone who has done more.)
duas.reference: (Abu Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Whoever says: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer.) a hundred times during the day, has the reward of freeing ten slaves, a hundred good deeds are recorded for him and a hundred bad deeds are wiped away and he has gained refuge from the devil that day until evening and none shall come with anything better except someone who has done more.)
duas.translation_fr: (Abou Hourayra a rapporté que le Messager d'Allah a dit : « Celui qui dit : (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah, seul, sans associé. À Lui appartiennent toute la souveraineté et la louange et Il est sur toutes choses tout-puissant.) cent fois pendant la journée, a la récompense de libérer dix esclaves, cent bonnes actions sont enregistrées pour lui et cent mauvaises actions sont effacées et il a trouvé refuge contre le diable ce jour-là jusqu'au soir et personne ne viendra avec quelque chose de mieux que celui qui a fait plus.)
duas.phonetic: Abu Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Whoever says: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer.) a hundred times during the day, has the reward of freeing ten slaves, a hundred good deeds are recorded for him and a hundred bad deeds are wiped away and he has gained refuge from the devil that day until evening and none shall come with anything better except someone who has done more.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((كَلِمَتَانِ خَفِيفَتَانِ عَلَى اللِّسَانِ، ثَقِيلَتَانِ فِي الْمِيزَانِ، حَبِيبَتَانِ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ، سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ)).
duas.translation_en: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:’(There are) Two words, (which are ) light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: (How perfect Allah is and I praise Him. How perfect Allah is, The Supreme.))
duas.reference: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:’(There are) Two words, (which are ) light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: (Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih, wasubhanal-lahil-AAatheem.))
duas.translation_fr: (Aboo Hurayrah a rapporté que le Messager d'Allah a dit : « (Il y a) deux mots, (qui sont) légers sur la langue, lourds sur la balance et aimés du Très Miséricordieux : (Comme Allah est parfait et je Le loue. Comme Allah est parfait, Le Suprême.))
duas.phonetic: Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:’(There are) Two words, (which are ) light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: (Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih, wasubhanal-lahil-AAatheem.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لَأَنْ أَقُولَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا طَلَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ الشَّمسُ)).
duas.translation_en: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Saying: (How perfect Allah is, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the greatest.) is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.)
duas.reference: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Saying: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, la ilaha illal-lah wallahu akbar.) is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.)
duas.translation_fr: (Aboo Hurayrah a rapporté que le Messager d'Allah a dit : « Dire : (Comme Allah est parfait, et toute louange est à Allah. Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah, et Allah est le plus grand.) m'est plus aimé que tout ce sur quoi le soleil s'est levé.)
duas.phonetic: Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Saying: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, la ilaha illal-lah wallahu akbar.) is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُم أَنْ يَكْسِبَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ)) فَسَأَلَهُ سَائِلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَائِهِ كَيْفَ يَكْسِبُ أَحَدُنَا أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: ((يُسَبِّحُ مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ، فَيُكتَبُ لَهُ أَلْفُ حَسَنَةٍ أَوْ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ أَلْفُ خَطِيئَةٍ)).
duas.translation_en: (Sa’d  said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah , and he said: ‘Are any of you unable to gain a thousand good deeds each day?’ Somebody then asked him  : How does one achieve a thousand good deeds? He replied: ‘He should say: (How perfect Allah is.) one hundred times, for a thousand good deeds are recorded for him or a thousand bad deeds are wiped away.)
duas.reference: (Sa’d  said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah , and he said: ‘Are any of you unable to gain a thousand good deeds each day?’ Somebody then asked him  : How does one achieve a thousand good deeds? He replied: ‘He should say: (Subhanal-lah.) one hundred times, for a thousand good deeds are recorded for him or a thousand bad deeds are wiped away.)
duas.translation_fr: (Sa'd a dit : « Nous étions assis avec le Messager d'Allah et il a dit : « L'un d'entre vous est-il incapable d'acquérir mille bonnes actions chaque jour ? » Quelqu'un lui a alors demandé : Comment peut-on accomplir mille bonnes actions ? Il a répondu : « Il devrait dire : (Comme Allah est parfait.) cent fois, car mille bonnes actions sont enregistrées pour lui ou mille mauvaises actions sont effacées.)
duas.phonetic: Sa’d  said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah , and he said: ‘Are any of you unable to gain a thousand good deeds each day?’ Somebody then asked him  : How does one achieve a thousand good deeds? He replied: ‘He should say: (Subhanal-lah.) one hundred times, for a thousand good deeds are recorded for him or a thousand bad deeds are wiped away.

duas.arabic: ((مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ غُرِسَتْ لَهُ نَخْلَةٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ)).
duas.translation_en: (Jabbir  related that the Prophet  said: ‘Whoever says: (How perfect Allah is . The Supreme, and I praise Him.) a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.)
duas.reference: (Jabbir  related that the Prophet  said: ‘Whoever says: (Subhanal-lahil-AAatheemi wabihamdih.) a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.)
duas.translation_fr: (Jabbir a rapporté que le Prophète a dit : « Celui qui dit : (Comme Allah est parfait. Le Suprême, et je Le loue.) un palmier est planté pour lui au paradis.)
duas.phonetic: Jabbir  related that the Prophet  said: ‘Whoever says: (Subhanal-lahil-AAatheemi wabihamdih.) a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ قَيْسٍ أَلاَ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى كَنْزٍ مِنْ كُنُوزِ الْجَنَّةِ))؟ فَقُلْتُ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: ((قُلْ لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ)).
duas.translation_en: (AAabdullah Ibn Qays  related that the Prophet  said to him: ‘ O ‘AAabdullah Ibn Qays, shall I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of paradise?’ He  then said: ‘Say: (There is no might nor power except with Allah.))
duas.reference: (AAabdullah Ibn Qays  related that the Prophet  said to him: ‘ O ‘AAabdullah Ibn Qays, shall I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of paradise?’ He  then said: ‘Say: (La  hawla wala quwwata illa billah.))
duas.translation_fr: (AAabdullah Ibn Qays a raconté que le Prophète lui a dit : « O 'AAabdullah Ibn Qays, ne devrais-je pas t'informer d'un trésor parmi les trésors du paradis ? » Il a ensuite dit : « Dis : (Il n'y a de force ni de pouvoir qu'en Allah.))
duas.phonetic: AAabdullah Ibn Qays  related that the Prophet  said to him: ‘ O ‘AAabdullah Ibn Qays, shall I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of paradise?’ He  then said: ‘Say: (La  hawla wala quwwata illa billah.

duas.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَحَبُّ الْكَلاَمِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَرْبَعٌ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لاَ يَضُرُّكَ بِأَيِّهِنَّ بَدَأتَ)).
duas.translation_en: (the most beloved words to Allah are four: (How perfect Allah is, all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah and Allah is the greatest.))(it does not matter which of them you start with.)
duas.reference: (the most beloved words to Allah are four: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, wala ilaha illal-lah, wallahu akbar.))(it does not matter which of them you start with.)
duas.translation_fr: (les paroles les plus aimées d'Allah sont au nombre de quatre : (Comme Allah est parfait, toute louange est à Allah. Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré sauf Allah et Allah est le plus grand.))(peu importe par lequel d'entre eux vous commencez.)
duas.phonetic: the most beloved words to Allah are four: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, wala ilaha illal-lah, wallahu akbar.))(it does not matter which of them you start with.

duas.arabic: جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَقَالَ: عَلِّمْنِي كَلاماً أَقُولُهُ: قَالَ: ((قُلْ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كَبِيراً، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَثِيراً، سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ، لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ)) قَالَ: فَهَؤُلاَءِ لِرَبِّي، فَمَا لِي؟ قَالَ: ((قُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي)).
duas.translation_en: (SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas  narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah  and said to him: ‘Teach me something which I should say?’ He said: ‘Say: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone without partener. Allah is most great and much praise is for Allah. How perfect Allah is, Lord of the worlds. There is no might nor power except with Allah, The Exalted in might, The Wise.) the man then said: ‘These are for my Lord, and what is for me?’ He  replied: ‘Say:(O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me and grant me sustenance.))
duas.reference: (SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas  narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah  and said to him: ‘Teach me something which I should say?’ He said: ‘Say: (La ilaha illal-lah, wahdahu la shareeka lah, Allahu akbaru kabeera, walhamdu lillahi katheera, subhanal-lahi rabbil-AAalameen, la hawla wala quwwata illa billahil-AAazeezil-hakeem.) the man then said: ‘These are for my Lord, and what is for me?’ He  replied: ‘Say:(Allahummagh-fir lee, warhamnee, wahdinee, warzuqnee.))
duas.translation_fr: (SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas a rapporté qu'un homme est venu vers le Messager d'Allah et lui a dit : « Apprends-moi quelque chose que je devrais dire ? » Il a dit : « Dis : (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré en dehors d'Allah, seul sans partenaire. Allah est le plus grand et la plus grande louange est à Allah. Combien Allah est parfait, Seigneur des mondes. Il n'y a de force ni de pouvoir qu'en Allah, le Puissant, le Sage.) l'homme dit alors : « Ceux-ci sont pour mon Seigneur, et qu'y a-t-il pour moi ? » Il répondit : « Dis : (Ô Allah, pardonne-moi, aie pitié de moi, guide-moi et accorde-moi de la nourriture.))
duas.phonetic: SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas  narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah  and said to him: ‘Teach me something which I should say?’ He said: ‘Say: (La ilaha illal-lah, wahdahu la shareeka lah, Allahu akbaru kabeera, walhamdu lillahi katheera, subhanal-lahi rabbil-AAalameen, la hawla wala quwwata illa billahil-AAazeezil-hakeem.) the man then said: ‘These are for my Lord, and what is for me?’ He  replied: ‘Say:(Allahummagh-fir lee, warhamnee, wahdinee, warzuqnee.

duas.arabic: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ عَلَّمَهُ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الصَّلاَةَ ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِهَؤُلاَءِ الْكَلِمَاتِ: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرِ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي)).
duas.translation_en: (Tariq Al-AshjaAAee  said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam, the Prophet  would teach him how to perform prayer and then order him to supplicate with the following words: (O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me, give me health and grant me sustenance.))
duas.reference: (Tariq Al-AshjaAAee  said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam, the Prophet  would teach him how to perform prayer and then order him to supplicate with the following words: (Allahummagh-fir lee, warhamnee, wahdinee, waAAafinee warzuqnee.))
duas.translation_fr: (Tariq Al-AshjaAAee a dit : « Quand quelqu'un acceptait l'Islam, le Prophète lui enseignait comment accomplir la prière et lui ordonnait ensuite d'invoquer avec les mots suivants : (Ô Allah, pardonne-moi, aie pitié de moi, guide-moi, donne-moi la santé et accorde-moi de la subsistance.))
duas.phonetic: Tariq Al-AshjaAAee  said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam, the Prophet  would teach him how to perform prayer and then order him to supplicate with the following words: (Allahummagh-fir lee, warhamnee, wahdinee, waAAafinee warzuqnee.

duas.arabic: ((إِنَّ أَفْضَلَ الدُّعَاءِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَأَفْضَلَ الذِّكْرِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ)).
duas.translation_en: (Jabir  Ibn ‘AAabdullah  related that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Verily, the best supplication is: (All praise is for Allah.) and indeed, the best form of remembrance is: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah.))
duas.reference: (Jabir  Ibn ‘AAabdullah  related that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Verily, the best supplication is: (Alhamdu lillah) and indeed, the best form of remembrance is: (La ilaha illal-lah.))
duas.translation_fr: (Jabir Ibn 'AAabdullah a rapporté que le Messager d'Allah a dit : « En vérité, la meilleure supplication est : (Toute louange est à Allah.) et en effet, la meilleure forme de souvenir est : (Nul n'a le droit d'être adoré en dehors d'Allah.))
duas.phonetic: Jabir  Ibn ‘AAabdullah  related that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Verily, the best supplication is: (Alhamdu lillah) and indeed, the best form of remembrance is: (La ilaha illal-lah.

duas.arabic: الباقيات الصالحات : سبحان الله والحمد لله ، ولا إله إلا الله ،والله أكبر ،و لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
duas.translation_en: The good deeds which endure are 'Glorified is Allah', and 'The praise is for Allah', and 'but There is none worthy of worship Allah', and 'Allah is the Most Great', and 'There is no power and no might except by Allah'
duas.reference: The good deeds which endure are 'Subhaanallaah.', and 'Walhamdulillaah.', and 'Wa laa ilaaha illallaah'., and 'Wallaahu Akbar',and 'Wa laa hawla wa laa guwawala illaa billaah'.
duas.translation_fr: Les bonnes actions qui durent sont « Glorifié soit Allah », et « La louange est à Allah », et « Mais il n'y a personne qui soit digne d'adorer Allah », et « Allah est le plus grand », et « Il n'y a de puissance ni de puissance que par Allah ».
duas.phonetic: albaqyat alsalhat : sbhan allh walhmd llh wla ilh ila allh wallh akbr w la hwl wla qwh ila ballh

title_en: How the Prophet glorified Allah
title_ar: كيف كان النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم يسبح ؟

duas.arabic: عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو رضي الله عنه قَالَ: ((رَأَيْتُ النَّبيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَعْقِدُ التَّسْبِيحَ)) وفي زيادةٍ: ((بِيَمِينِهِ)).
duas.translation_en: (Tasbeeh, it means here, to say: (Subhanal-lah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar.)) (AAabdullah Ibn AAamr  said: ’I saw the prophet  make tasbeeh with his right hand.)
duas.translation_fr: (Tasbeeh, cela signifie ici, dire : (Subhanal-lah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar.)) (AAabdullah Ibn AAamr a dit : 'J'ai vu le prophète faire le tasbeeh avec sa main droite.)
duas.phonetic: 'an 'abdi allahi bni 'amrinw rdy allh 'nh qaala: ((raaaytu alnabya sla allh 'lyh wslm ya'qidu altasbiyha)) wfy zyadhin: ((biyamiynihi)).
